title: [podman] database configuration mismatch on --tmpdir
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Setting _tmpdir_ on CLI creates a _database configuration mismatch_ even if there's no database.
Might be related to https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1853

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `rm -rf /home/podman/rundir/ /tmp/libpod-rundir-1001/ /home/podman/.local/share/containers`
2. `mkdir /tmp/libpod-tmpdir-1001/`
3. 
```
~/podman/usr/local/bin/podman --log-level=debug --conmon /home/podman/podman/usr/local/libexec/crio/conmon --network-cmd-path /home/podman/podman/usr/local/bin/slirp4netns --runtime /home/podman/podman/usr/local/sbin/runc --runroot /tmp/libpod-rundir-1001/ --tmpdir /tmp/libpod-tmpdir-$UID run busybox sh
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```
INFO[0000] running as rootless
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver vfs
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage
DEBU[0000] Using run root /tmp/libpod-rundir-1001/
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /tmp/libpod-tmpdir-1001
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
ERRO[0000] error creating libpod runtime: database libpod temporary files directory (tmpdir) /tmp/libpod-rundir-1001/tmp does not match our libpod temporary files directory (tmpdir) /tmp/libpod-tmpdir-1001: database configuration mismatch
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
INFO[0000] running as rootless
Version:            1.2.0-dev

Git Commit:         c7aa191c8bcb83e8f5064d458d6604fcdf0e41d9

OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "vfs"

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Debian 9 on VirtualBox 6 on Windows 10 on AMD 64

comments.body: Alright, this one's properly confusing. You deleted the database, and yet we're still complaining about the database's configuration not matching.
